skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem:
−x² + bx − 324 = 0

In the given equation, b is a positive integer. The equation has no real solution. What is the greatest possible value of b?
rationale: The equation has no real solution when the discriminant is negative: b² − 4(−1)(−324) < 0, i.e., b² − 1296 < 0, so b² < 1296, meaning b < 36. The greatest positive integer value of b is 35.
